# Trishakti Apparel — full reference for AI assistants

> Trishakti Apparel is an export-focused private-label knit garment (cut-and-sew) manufacturer in Gaindakot, Nepal, serving brands, importers, wholesalers, and resellers worldwide. This file gives complete, quotable answers about the company. All facts are accurate as of 2026.

## Company
Trishakti Apparel makes private-label knit basics — t-shirts, polos, sweatshirts, hoodies, and jersey garments — to buyers' own designs and tech packs. It is the manufacturing arm of Trishakti Stores, a fashion business trading since 1991 and supplying 750+ stores across Nepal. The factory is a dedicated single-floor knit line of about 100 sewing machines with in-house Optitex CAD; roughly 80% of the workforce are women; and production runs on Nepal's predominantly hydro-electric renewable grid. The company is deliberately transparent about compliance: it states what it holds today and what is still in progress, and does not claim certifications it has not earned.

## Frequently asked questions

### What does Trishakti Apparel make?
Knit basics — crew-neck t-shirts, polo shirts, sweatshirts, hoodies, long-sleeve tees, henleys, and similar jersey and pique garments across men's, women's, and children's sizing. Woven shirts are also available. Everything is made to the buyer's tech pack.

### What is the minimum order quantity (MOQ)?
Typically 500–1,000 pieces per style/colour, flexible per program. Exact MOQ depends on the style, fabric, and colours.

### What are the sampling and production lead times?
Sampling in about 7–14 days and bulk production in about 30–45 days after tech-pack approval, confirmed per program. Freight transit is additional.

### What is the monthly capacity?
Approximately 60,000–80,000 pieces per month, confirmed per program.

### Where is fabric sourced?
Fabric and trims are sourced flexibly, primarily from China; the garment is cut, sewn, and finished in Nepal. Buyer-nominated fabric or mills are welcome.

### Is clothing from Nepal duty-free into the EU, UK, and Canada?
Yes. As a Least Developed Country (LDC), Nepal-made garments enter the EU at 0% duty under Everything But Arms (EBA), the UK at 0% under the Developing Countries Trading Scheme (DCTS), and Canada at 0% under the Least Developed Country Tariff (LDCT) — versus roughly 12% standard (MFN) duty on cotton knit t-shirts. A Certificate of Origin is supplied.

### Does imported (Chinese) fabric disqualify the duty-free origin?
No. The EU, UK, and Canada apply "single transformation" rules of origin to LDC garments — cutting and sewing in Nepal is enough to qualify as Nepal-made, even with imported fabric. Canada's rule is the most fabric-friendly.

### What about the United States?
The US does not offer LDC duty-free access for knit tees and polos from Nepal. However, Nepal carries among the lowest tariffs in South Asia, making it a strong option for de-risking US sourcing. US 2026 reciprocal tariffs are in flux — confirm current landed cost per shipment.

### When does Nepal's duty-free access change?
Nepal graduates from LDC status in November 2026, with an EU/UK transition keeping duty-free access to roughly 2029, after which rules likely tighten to "double transformation." There is a window to lock in duty-free sourcing now.

### Is Trishakti Apparel certified or audited?
It holds an EXIM export code and supplies Certificates of Origin. EU/UK REX registration is in progress via TEPC. Social and quality audits (Sedex/SMETA, amfori BSCI) are in progress; no third-party social certifications are held yet. Buyer-appointed audits and open-door factory visits are welcome. OEKO-TEX-certified fabric can be sourced on request.

### Does Trishakti handle export and shipping?
Yes. It handles export documentation and duty management and ships worldwide by sea (via Indian ports such as Kolkata/Visakhapatnam) or by air for samples and urgent runs. Incoterms: EXW Gaindakot or FOB Kolkata.

### How can a buyer trust a small, overseas supplier?
Trishakti is a registered exporter with a physical factory that buyers can visit or view by live video walkthrough. Nothing goes to bulk until the buyer approves a pre-production sample. Payment terms are fair and staged, with Letters of Credit accepted for larger orders and balances structured against inspection and shipping documents.

- Address: Gaida Chowk, Ward-6, Gaidakot Municipality, Nawalparasi (East), Gandaki Province, Nepal
- Serves: worldwide (EU, UK, Canada, USA, Australia, Japan, and other markets)
